seo.title: Building Content Generation with LangGraph + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build content generation with LangGraph + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building content generation workflows with LangGraph enables sophisticated LLM-powered pipelines for creating articles, posts, and marketing copy with multi-step refinement. However, complex graph cycles, state merges, and schema evolution introduce risks like infinite loops, factual inaccuracies, and brand voice drift. Vex provides essential guardrails to verify brand consistency, factual accuracy, and editorial compliance at critical checkpoints.

architectureOverview.heading: Content Generation Architecture with LangGraph
architectureOverview.description:
A typical LangGraph content generation workflow starts with a 'Researcher' node that gathers facts and SEO keywords via web search and LLM summarization. Parallel 'Writer' and 'Brand Voice Generator' nodes then produce draft content and style guidelines. These merge into an 'Editor' node for fact-checking and tone alignment, followed by a 'Fact-Checker' that validates claims against sources. A human-in-the-loop 'Reviewer' node handles edge cases, with conditional edges routing back to rewrite if quality thresholds fail.

The graph maintains persistent state with checkpoints containing draft text, source citations, SEO scores, and brand compliance metrics. Cyclical edges enable iterative refinement until all verification criteria pass. This architecture handles complex content like blog posts with embedded research, multimedia suggestions, and A/B headline variants.

Vex integrates at merge points, pre-human-review, and final output to catch brand drift, factual errors, and SEO violations before deployment.

risks.name: Cyclic graph execution causing infinite state transitions
risks.description: Researcher-Writer-Editor loops without convergence criteria cause endless rewrites when LLM outputs consistently fail brand voice checks
risks.mitigation: Add Vex verification with max_iterations counter and convergence scoring before cycle edges

risks.name: State corruption when parallel branches merge conflicting data
risks.description: Brand Voice Generator and Writer produce incompatible tone instructions, corrupting merged state with contradictory style directives
risks.mitigation: Use Vex to validate merge compatibility and resolve conflicts with priority-weighted reconciliation

risks.name: Checkpoint deserialization errors after schema changes
risks.description: Adding SEO compliance fields breaks loading of historical checkpoints, losing draft progress during iterative content refinement
risks.mitigation: Implement Vex schema validation and backward-compatible state migration at checkpoint boundaries

risks.name: Human-in-the-loop breakpoints bypassed by conditional edges
risks.description: High-confidence fact-checks skip human review, publishing unverified content that violates editorial guidelines
risks.mitigation: Vex enforces mandatory human breakpoints for medium+ risk content regardless of automated confidence scores

implementation.title: Basic Content Generation Graph
implementation.description: Core LangGraph workflow with researcher, writer, and basic state management for content creation
implementation.code:
from typing import TypedDict, Annotated
from langgraph.graph import StateGraph, END
from langgraph.graph.message import add_messages

class ContentState(TypedDict):
    topic: str
    research: str
    draft: str
    sources: list[str]
    
def researcher(state: ContentState) -> ContentState:
    return {'research': f'Research on {state["topic"]}: key facts...', 'sources': ['source1.com']}

def writer(state: ContentState) -> ContentState:
    return {'draft': f'Draft on {state["topic"]} using {state["research"][:100]}...'}

workflow = StateGraph(ContentState)
workflow.add_node('researcher', researcher)
workflow.add_node('writer', writer)
workflow.set_entry_point('researcher')
workflow.add_edge('researcher', 'writer')
workflow.add_edge('writer', END)

app = workflow.compile()
result = app.invoke({'topic': 'AI content generation'})

implementation.title: Parallel Brand Voice + Content Generation
implementation.description: Fan-out to generate content and brand guidelines simultaneously, then merge results
implementation.code:
from langgraph.prebuilt import ToolNode, tools_condition
from langgraph.graph import StateGraph

class ContentState(TypedDict):
    topic: str
    draft: str
    brand_voice: str
    merged_content: str

async def brand_voice_gen(state: ContentState) -> ContentState:
    return {'brand_voice': 'Professional, approachable tone with tech-forward language'}

async def content_writer(state: ContentState) -> ContentState:
    return {'draft': f'Article draft with {state["topic"]} in brand voice'}

workflow = StateGraph(ContentState)
workflow.add_node('brand_voice', brand_voice_gen)
workflow.add_node('writer', content_writer)

workflow.set_entry_point('brand_voice')
workflow.add_edge('brand_voice', 'writer')

# Merge logic
async def merge_branches(state: ContentState) -> ContentState:
    return {
        'merged_content': f'{state["draft"]}\nStyle: {state["brand_voice"]}'
    }

workflow.add_node('merge', merge_branches)
workflow.add_edge('writer', 'merge')
workflow.add_edge('merge', END)

app = workflow.compile()

implementation.title: Fact-Checking with External Validation
implementation.description: Add fact-checking node that validates claims against external sources before final output
implementation.code:
import re

class ContentState(TypedDict):
    draft: str
    facts: list[str]
    verified: bool
    citations: dict

async def extract_and_check_facts(state: ContentState) -> ContentState:
    # Extract factual claims
    facts = re.findall(r'(\w+ (is|are|was|will be) \w+)', state['draft'])
    
    verified_facts = []
    citations = {}
    
    for fact in facts[:3]:  # Limit for demo
        # Mock external validation
        verified = fact[0] in ['AI is powerful', 'Python is popular']
        verified_facts.append((fact[0], verified))
        if verified:
            citations[fact[0]] = f'https://source-for-{fact[0].lower().replace(" ", "-")}.com'
    
    return {
        'facts': verified_facts,
        'verified': all(v for _, v in verified_facts),
        'citations': citations
    }

# Add to existing workflow
workflow.add_node('fact_check', extract_and_check_facts)
workflow.add_conditional_edges(
    'merge',
    lambda state: 'fact_check' if not state.get('verified', False) else END,
    {'fact_check': 'fact_check', END: END}
)
workflow.add_edge('fact_check', END)

implementation.title: Human-in-the-Loop with Breakpoints
implementation.description: Implement conditional human review for content failing automated quality thresholds
implementation.code:
from langgraph.checkpoint.memory import MemorySaver

class ContentState(TypedDict):
    draft: str
    quality_score: float
    needs_human_review: bool
    human_feedback: str

async def quality_assessment(state: ContentState) -> ContentState:
    # Mock LLM quality scoring
    score = len(state['draft']) / 1000.0  # Simplified
    needs_review = score < 0.7
    
    return {
        'quality_score': score,
        'needs_human_review': needs_review
    }

def should_review(state: ContentState):
    return 'human_review' if state['needs_human_review'] else END

async def human_review(state: ContentState):
    print(f"🚨 HUMAN REVIEW NEEDED: Score {state['quality_score']:.2f}")
    print(f"Draft: {state['draft'][:200]}...")
    return {'human_feedback': 'Approved with minor edits'}

# Checkpointing for HITL
checkpointer = MemorySaver()

workflow.add_node('quality_check', quality_assessment)
workflow.add_node('human_review', human_review)

workflow.add_conditional_edges('merge', should_review)
workflow.add_edge('quality_check', 'human_review')
workflow.add_edge('human_review', END)

app = workflow.compile(checkpointer=checkpointer)

vexIntegration.title: Wrap graph.invoke() with Vex.verify()
vexIntegration.description: Simplest integration - verify final output against all content quality criteria

vexIntegration.title: Vex as Graph Verification Node
vexIntegration.description: Add Vex as an explicit node in the workflow for intermediate verification
vexIntegration.code:
import vex
from typing import Any

async def vex_content_verifier(state: ContentState) -> ContentState:
    """Vex verification node for content quality"""
    
    checks = {
        "brand_consistency": {
            "type": "llm_rubric",
            "rubric": "Does this match our professional, innovative brand voice?",
            "threshold": "PASS"
        },
        "originality": {
            "type": "plagiarism_check",
            "min_originality": 90
        },
        "tone_adherence": {
            "type": "sentiment_analysis",
            "target": "confident_professional"
        }
    }
    
    result = vex_client.verify(
        input=state['merged_content'],
        checks=checks
    )
    
    state['vex_scores'] = result['scores']
    state['verification_passed'] = result['passed']
    
    if not result['passed']:
        state['needs_revision'] = True
        print("🔍 Vex flagged issues:", result['failures'])
    
    return state

# Add to workflow
workflow.add_node('vex_verify', vex_content_verifier)

# Route through verification
workflow.add_edge('merge', 'vex_verify')
workflow.add_conditional_edges(
    'vex_verify',
    lambda state: 'human_review' if not state['verification_passed'] else END
)

vexIntegration.title: Merge State Validation with Vex
vexIntegration.description: Prevent state corruption by validating parallel branch merges before proceeding
